health.mental_health: High stress and anxiety tied to housing instability and inconsistent sleep; occasional panic-like symptoms when dealing with landlord notices, no formal diagnosis documented.
recent_visits: Urgent care visit for asthma flare during a cold; received nebulizer treatment and inhaler refill
Physical therapy evaluation for knee pain and shoulder instability; home exercise plan provided
Primary care visit to review migraine frequency and discuss triggers/sleep
Walk-in clinic visit for work note after migraine-related missed shift

finance.debt: Behind on rent by about 1–2 months after several reduced-hour weeks; carries ~$3,800 in credit card debt and a small medical payment plan for PT/urgent care copays; received a notice to quit and is trying to negotiate a payment arrangement.
investments: No formal investments; has a small employer 401(k) but contributions paused
Small savings buffer in a basic checking/savings account (often under $500)
recent_transactions: Partial rent payment to landlord with memo 'payment plan' (short of full amount)
Pharmacy copay for inhaler and migraine medication
Money order fee for rent payment
Small cash withdrawal for laundry and groceries

relationships: On-and-off support from her mother (lives out of state) who checks in about eviction risk
Close friend and former roommate, Alyssa Chen, who sometimes lets Kavya shower/do laundry and store a bag
Coworker confidante, Marisol Rivera, who occasionally swaps shifts when Kavya has migraines or housing appointments
Estranged relationship with an ex-partner who is still on an old lease document and occasionally contacts her
social.social_media_activity: Low-key presence; mostly follows local Boston groups and posts occasional photos from the Common/harbor walk. Avoids posting about housing issues; uses Facebook groups to look for rooms and sublets under a different privacy setting.
private_conversations: Texting a friend about whether she can crash on a couch if the eviction goes through
Messages with landlord about arrears, payment plan, and inspection dates
Calls with a legal aid intake line about tenant rights and court timeline
Discussing migraine frequency and missed work with a coworker to cover shifts

schedule.work_hours: Typically 32–40 hours/week with early mornings; common shifts are 7:00am–3:00pm or 8:00am–4:00pm, plus occasional weekend coverage depending on client needs.
regular_appointments: Physical therapy for knee/shoulder (every other week when copays are manageable)
Primary care follow-up (every 6 months; more often if asthma worsens)
Housing court/legal aid check-ins (as scheduled during eviction process)
upcoming_events: Meeting with landlord/property manager about repayment plan and lease renewal decision
Legal aid clinic appointment for eviction paperwork review
PT session focused on knee stability and shoulder strengthening
Work in-service training on infection control (mandatory)
schedule.daily_routine: Up early to commute by MBTA to client homes; tries to prep simple meals and pack meds/inhaler; evenings are split between recovery (ice/brace for knee), job paperwork, and housing-related calls/forms; sleep is inconsistent when stress or migraines hit.

professional.company: A Boston-area home care agency contracted with MassHealth and private pay clients (e.g., a mid-sized agency in the Greater Boston area)
projects: Supporting an elderly client with morning ADLs, meal prep, and medication reminders (non-clinical)
Coordinating with a visiting nurse and family caregiver on safe transfers and fall prevention
Completing required documentation in the agency app and maintaining timesheets
Renewing CPR/First Aid certification and completing annual compliance modules
professional.performance_issues: Occasional late arrivals due to MBTA delays and unstable housing logistics; has missed a couple of shifts from migraines/asthma flare-ups and is worried about attendance points affecting assignment stability.
workplace_relationships: Generally good rapport with a scheduler who tries to cluster visits to reduce her commute
Tension with one client’s adult child who questions her timing and documentation
Relies on a small group of aides for shift swaps when health or housing appointments conflict

hobbies: Walking along the Charles River and the Emerald Necklace when her knee allows
Listening to podcasts/audiobooks on the T
Cooking simple South Asian-inspired meals when she has a stable kitchen access
Free community fitness/stretch classes (modified due to ACL/shoulder)
habits: Keeps an inhaler in her work bag and a backup in a coat pocket
Tracks migraines and triggers (sleep, stress, skipped meals) in a notes app
Uses a knee brace on longer shifts and ices after work
Limits spending by shopping at discount grocers and using food pantry resources occasionally
Avoids inviting people over due to housing situation and embarrassment about instability
lifestyle.living_situation: Currently renting a room in a shared apartment in Boston; behind on rent and at risk of eviction. Has intermittently stayed with a friend for a night or two when conflicts with roommates/landlord escalate, and keeps important documents in a grab-and-go folder.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly pescatarian/vegetarian at home, caffeine limited because it can trigger migraines; prioritizes low-cost, easy-prep foods and tends to skip meals during long shifts or high-stress days.
